---
title: Hydrophone Specs & Manufacturers
---

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple" style="color: #d32f2f; border-left-color: #d32f2f; background-color: #ffebee;"}
## Improvements for this page
* Fill remaining Hydrophone specs
:::


Ocean Networks Canada uses hydrophones from several manufacturers and distributors: the main ones being *Ocean Sonics* and *JASCO Applied Sciences*.

Most hydrophones are Ocean Sonics icListen smart hydrophones: icListen HF (High Frequency; 10 Hz to 200k Hz), and icListen AF (Audio Frequency; 10 Hz to 12 kHz).
JASCO provides Geospectrum-manufactured hydrophones for multi-element directional arrays, great for localizing sources.


```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
import pandas as pd
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# 1. Load the data
df_raw = pd.read_excel('../docs/ONC Hydrophones Specs.xlsx')

# 2. Replace empty cells to prevent JSON crashes
df_raw = df_raw.fillna("--")

# 3. Clean column names but KEEP duplicates distinct!
clean_columns = []
for i, col in enumerate(df_raw.columns):
    name = str(col)
    if "Unnamed" in name:
        clean_columns.append(f"Field_{i}")
    else:
        clean_columns.append(name)

df_raw.columns = clean_columns

# 4. Bridge the data
my_data = df_raw.to_dict(orient='records')
ojs_define(specs_data = my_data)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// 1. SAFELY extract the columns
all_cols = {
  if (specs_data && specs_data.length > 0) {
    return Object.keys(specs_data[0]);
  } else {
    return [];
  }
}

// 2. Define our labels based on the safe extraction
label_col = all_cols.length > 0 ? all_cols[0] : ""
model_cols = all_cols.length > 0 ? all_cols.slice(1) : []

// Extract the "Device" row (first row) and separate it from the rest of the specs
deviceRow = specs_data.length > 0 ? specs_data[0] : {}
specRows = specs_data.length > 1 ? specs_data.slice(1) : []

// 3. Map manufacturers to colors (Fixed naming mismatch to match file logic)
brandColors = ({
  "JASCO": "darkblue",         // Warm Amber
  "Ocean Sonics": "orange",  // Marine Deep Blue
  "Naxys": "purple",         // Deep Indigo/Purple
  "Feature": "darkgrey"        // Charcoal Slate for parameters
})

// Helper function to dynamically check column string headers
function getBrandColor(m) {
  if (m.includes("JASCO")) return brandColors.JASCO;
  if (m.includes("Ocean")) return brandColors["Ocean Sonics"];
  if (m.includes("Naxys")) return brandColors.Naxys;
  return "#475569";
}

// 4. IMPROVEMENT: Inline pill/tag selection UI that removes bulky checkboxes
viewof selectedModels = Inputs.checkbox(
  model_cols, 
  { 
    label: html`<span style="font-size: 1.1rem; color: #1e293b; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; display: block;">Select Models to Compare:</span>`, 
    value: [model_cols[0], model_cols[3], model_cols[4]], // Default layout
    format: x => deviceRow[x] && deviceRow[x] !== "--" ? deviceRow[x] : x.split('.')[0]
  }
)

// 5. Render the styled table!
html`
<style>
  /* Modernizing the Observable default checkboxes into a horizontal button bar */
  div.oi-3a0a3a {
    display: flex !important;
    flex-wrap: wrap !important;
    gap: 8px !important;
    padding: 12px 16px;
    background: #f8fafc;
    border-radius: 12px;
    border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
  }
  div.oi-3a0a3a label {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    background: white;
    padding: 6px 14px !important;
    border-radius: 20px !important;
    border: 1px solid #cbd5e1 !important;
    font-size: 0.875rem !important;
    font-weight: 500 !important;
    color: #475569 !important;
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: all 0.2s ease;
    margin: 0 !important;
  }
  div.oi-3a0a3a label:hover {
    background: #f1f5f9;
    border-color: #94a3b8 !important;
  }
  div.oi-3a0a3a input[type="checkbox"] {
    margin-right: 8px !important;
    accent-color: #0284C7;
  }
  
  /* Table Container */
  .table-responsive {
    overflow-x: auto; 
    overflow-y: auto;
    max-height: 600px;
    border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
    border-radius: 12px;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05);
  }
  
  .branded-table {
    border-collapse: separate;
    border-spacing: 0;
    width: 100%;
    font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
  }
  
  /* Fixed Headers styling */
  .branded-table th {
    padding: 16px 12px;
    color: white;
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 0.95rem;
    text-align: center;
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 10;
    box-shadow: inset 0 -2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
  }
  
  /* Parameters row header sticky to the left side */
  .branded-table td:first-child, .branded-table th:first-child {
    position: sticky;
    left: 0;
    z-index: 5;
    background-color: #f1f5f9;
    font-weight: 600;
    text-align: left;
    border-right: 2px solid #e2e8f0;
    min-width: 220px;
  }
  
  .branded-table th:first-child {
    z-index: 15; /* Top-left corner stays above everything */
  }

  .branded-table td {
    padding: 12px 16px;
    text-align: center;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    color: #334155;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
    background-color: #ffffff;
    min-width: 160px; 
  }
  
  /* Alternating Row Tint */
  .branded-table tr:nth-child(even) td:not(:first-child) {
    background-color: #f8fafc;
  }
  
  /* Custom highlights for Manufacturer Row */
  .mfg-row td {
    font-weight: 700 !important;
    color: #1e293b !important;
    background-color: #ecf1f6 !important;
    border-bottom: 2px solid #cbd5e1;
  }
</style>

<div class="table-responsive">
  <table class="branded-table">
    <thead>
      <tr>
        <th style="background-color: ${brandColors.Feature};">Specification</th>
        ${selectedModels.map(m => {
          let deviceName = deviceRow[m] && deviceRow[m] !== "--" ? deviceRow[m] : m.split('.')[0];
          return html`<th style="background-color: ${getBrandColor(m)}">${deviceName}</th>`;
        })}
      </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
      <tr class="mfg-row">
        <td>Manufacturer/Supplier</td>
        ${selectedModels.map(m => html`<td>${m.split('.')[0]}</td>`)}
      </tr>

      ${specRows.map(row => html`
        <tr>
          <td>${row[label_col]}</td>
          ${selectedModels.map(m => {
            let val = row[m];
            let style = val === "--" ? "color: #94a3b8; font-style: italic;" : "";
            return html`<td style="${style}">${val}</td>`;
          })}
        </tr>
      `)}
    </tbody>
  </table>
</div>
`
```


# Information about suppliers and manufacturers

## JASCO Applied Sciences [{{< fa link >}}](https://www.jasco.com/)
A scientific consulting firm and equipment supplier specializing in underwater acoustics.
Founded by Joseph Arnold Scrimger in Victoria, British Columbia, in 1981, the company now has its largest primary operations office in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada.

JASCO operates globally across national defense, marine environmental stewardship, oil and gas asset monitoring, and academic research.
They also perform acoustic modeling, field data collection, and advanced analysis suites.

Their *Autonomous Multichannel Acoustic Recorder (AMAR)* provides passive acoustic data acquisition with high bandwidth and low noise. 

Their hydrophones are manufactured by GeoSpectrum Technologies Inc.

## GeoSpectrum Technologies Inc. (GTI) [{{< fa link >}}](https://www.geospectrum.ca/)
A specialized Canadian aerospace and defense company, based in Halifax, NS, that focuses on the design, engineering, and manufacturing of underwater acoustic transducers and specialized systems.
GeoSpectrum hydrophones are high-performance, and frequently deployed as part of hydrophone arrays managed by JASCO.

## Ocean Sonics [{{< fa link >}}](https://oceansonicsinc.com/)
Ocean Sonics is a canadian, Nova Scotia-based pioneer in the development of smart digital hydrophones, found in 2005.
They have developed both low-frequency (icListenLF/AF) and high-frequency (icListenHF) hydrophones.

## Wildlife Acoustics [{{< fa link >}}](https://www.wildlifeacoustics.com/)
A US-based private company based in Maynar, MA specialized provider of bioacoustics monitoring technology founded in 2003 by Ian Agranat.
Their primary focus is on designing autonomous underwater recorders and sound analysis software for ecosystem biodiversity monitoring, environmental assessments, and wildlife presence/absence surveys.

Their hydrophone Song Meter SM2M, released in 2011, is designed for long-term autonomous deployments.
